data.title: Barclays latest British lender to quit climate banking alliance
data.description:
Barclays has become the latest British lender to quit the Net Zero Banking Alliance, the bank said on Friday, arguing that the departure of several global lenders has left it no longer fit to support the bank's green transition.

Barclays' decision to quit the foremost banking alliance focused on tackling climate change follows that of HSBC and several major U.S. banks and raises questions about the ability of the group to influence change in the sector going forward.

"After consideration, we have decided to withdraw from the Net Zero Banking Alliance," the bank said in a statement on its website.

The bank added that its commitment to be net zero by 2050 remained unchanged and that it still saw a commercial opportunity for itself and its clients in the energy transition.

Earlier this week Barclays published the first update on its sustainability strategy in several years and said it made 500 million pounds ($666.20 million) in revenue from sustainable and low-carbon transition finance in 2024.

Jeanne Martin, co-director of corporate engagement at responsible investment NGO ShareAction called the decision to leave the Net Zero Banking Alliance "incredibly disappointing and a step in the wrong direction at a time when the dangers of climate change are rapidly mounting."

Barclays said the alliance was no longer fit for its purpose: "With the departure of most of the global banks, the organisation no longer has the membership to support our transition."

The Net Zero Banking Alliance, a global initiative launched by the United Nations Environment Programme Finance Initiative, lists more than 100 members on its website, including leading international financial institutions.

A spokesperson for the alliance said it remains focused on "supporting its members to lead on climate by addressing the barriers preventing their clients from investing in the net-zero transition."

The NZBA earlier this year overhauled its rules after the departure of key members with banks voting to ditch some of its more stringent membership rules.

About Vodafone

Vodafone is a leading European and African telecoms company.

We serve over 355 million mobile and broadband customers, operating networks in 15 countries with investments in a further five and partners in over 40 more. Our undersea cables transport around a sixth of the world's internet traffic, and we are developing a new direct-to-mobile satellite communications service to connect areas without coverage. Vodafone runs one of the world's largest IoT platforms, with over 215 million IoT connections, and we provide financial services to around 92 million customers across seven African countries - managing more transactions than any other provider.

From the seabed to the stars, Vodafone's purpose is to keep everyone connected.

For more information, please visit www.vodafone.com follow us on X at @VodafoneGroup or connect with us on LinkedIn at www.linkedin.com/company/vodafone.

data.title: Ingersoll Rand Down Over 11%, on Track for Largest Percent Decrease Since March 2020 — Data Talk
data.description:
Ingersoll Rand Inc. (IR) is currently at $75.02, down $9.61 or 11.36%

  • Would be lowest close since April 29, 2025, when it closed at $74.86
  • On pace for largest percent decrease since March 9, 2020, when it fell 16.05%
  • Down 15.01% this week; worst weekly performance since the week ending March 13, 2020, when it fell 17.24%
  • Currently down five consecutive days; down 15.01% over this period
  • Longest losing streak since Dec. 19, 2024, when it fell for eight straight trading days
  • Worst five day stretch since the five days ending April 8, 2025, when it fell 17.19%
  • Down 17.07% year-to-date
  • Down 28.79% from its all-time closing high of $105.35 on Nov. 25, 2024
  • Down 14.18% from 52 weeks ago (Aug. 2, 2024), when it closed at $87.42
  • Down 28.79% from its 52-week closing high of $105.35 on Nov. 25, 2024
  • Up 12.39% from its 52-week closing low of $66.75 on April 8, 2025
  • Traded as low as $74.54; lowest intraday level since April 30, 2025, when it hit $72.73
  • Down 11.92% at today's intraday low; largest intraday percent decrease since March 18, 2020, when it fell as much as 21.03%
  • Third worst performer in the S&P 500 today

All data as of 10:47:21 AM ET

Source: Dow Jones Market Data, FactSet

data.title: Imperial Oil's Good Quarter Likely Not Enough for Share to Outperform — Market Talk
data.description: Overall, Imperial Oil had a good quarter, reckons Raymond James's Michael Barth. The analyst says the energy company managed a modest 4% beat on funds from operations over consensus estimates on what largely appears to be slightly stronger production. Still, given that Imperial is one of the best performing large-cap Canadian energy names, Barth is skeptical the latest quarter will be enough to drive meaningful outperformance against its peer group from here. Shares are down 1% at C$114.34. (robb.stewart@wsj.com)

data.title: GOLD ROCK HOLDINGS, INC. SEC 10-Q Report
data.description:
Gold Rock Holdings, Inc., a company specializing in AI services and Web3 content management systems, has released its Form 10-Q report for the second quarter of 2025. The report highlights substantial financial growth and strategic business developments, driven primarily by the performance of its K-Project AI division and the LOOT8, Inc. subsidiary.

Financial Highlights

Sales: The company reported sales of $70,800 for the three months ended June 30, 2025, a significant increase from $6,500 for the same period in 2024. This growth is attributed to the strong performance of the K-Project AI division.

Gross Profit: Gross profit for the three months ended June 30, 2025, was $70,800, with a gross margin of 100%, indicating no cost of goods sold during this period.

Net Income (Loss) for the Period: The company achieved a net income of $15,385 for the three months ended June 30, 2025, compared to a net loss of $92,999 for the same period in 2024, demonstrating improved profitability.

Net Income (Loss) for the Period Per Common Shares - Basic and Diluted: The net income per common share was $0.00 for the three months ended June 30, 2025, compared to $(0.00) for the same period in 2024, indicating a break-even per-share performance.

Business Highlights

Revenue Segments: The K-Project division generated $70,800 in revenue from AI services from one customer in the three months ended June 30, 2025, and $136,800 in the six months ended June 30, 2025. The LOOT8, Inc. subsidiary contributed $1,500 in revenue from its Web3 content management system during the same period.

New Product Launches: In January 2025, LOOT8, Inc. launched a 'Singer and Song Writer Contest' on its Web3 social media platform, showcasing the platform's capabilities. Additionally, the K-Project division developed the SAID (Speech Artificial Intelligence On Demand) translation application, which offers instant translations across approximately 100 languages without internet connectivity.

Future Outlook: Gold Rock Holdings, Inc. plans to expand its LOOT8 platform, focusing on blockchain innovation in digital assets and SocialFi. The K-Project division aims to enhance its AI software solutions, targeting various industries such as healthcare, law enforcement, and education. The company is also exploring new revenue streams through direct-to-consumer models and enterprise solutions.

Operational Strategy: The company intends to grow through mergers, acquisitions, and management of technological assets. It is focusing on developing AI agent applications and leveraging its Web3 content management system to empower individuals with monetization opportunities beyond traditional models.

Management Changes: Anthony Denkinger was appointed as Chief Operations Officer of Gold Rock Holdings, Inc. in February 2025, while continuing as CEO of the subsidiary LOOT8, Inc. This reflects the company's strategic focus on strengthening its operational leadership.

SEC Filing: GOLD ROCK HOLDINGS, INC. [ GRHI ] - 10-Q - Aug. 01, 2025

data.title: CPS TECHNOLOGIES CORP/DE/ SEC 10-Q Report
data.description:
CPS Technologies Corporation, a leading provider of advanced material solutions, has released its Form 10-Q report for the second quarter of 2024. The report highlights significant improvements in both financial and operational performance, driven by increased demand across various product lines and enhanced production efficiency.

Financial Highlights

Total Revenue: $8.08 million, marking a 61% increase compared to Q2 2024. This growth is attributed to rising demand in other product lines and additional funding under the federal government’s SBIR program.

Gross Profit: $1.34 million, representing 17% of sales, compared to a gross loss of $0.23 million or -5% of sales in Q2 2024. The improvement is driven by higher revenues and better production yields.

Operating Income: $0.14 million, a significant turnaround from an operating loss of $1.31 million in Q2 2024. This reflects an increased gross margin despite higher SG&A expenses.

Net Income: $0.10 million, compared to a net loss of $0.95 million in Q2 2024. The positive net income is attributed to increased revenue and improved operational efficiency.

Net Income Per Basic Common Share: $0.01, compared to a loss of $0.07 per share in Q2 2024, indicating a return to profitability.

Business Highlights

Product Lines Performance: CPS Technologies Corporation provides advanced material solutions, primarily metal-matrix composites (MMC), to various industries including electronics, power generation, automotive, and defense. The company also assembles housings and packages for hybrid circuits, which may include MMC components or traditional materials like aluminum.

Operational Efficiency: The company added a third shift in September 2024 to meet growing demand across its product lines, indicating an increase in production capacity and operational efficiency.

Research and Development: CPS engages in both government-funded and internally funded R&D to develop new products in response to customer requirements, expanding offerings in existing markets and enabling penetration into new markets.

Future Outlook: The company anticipates continued growth in demand for MMC, housings for hybrid circuits, and its proprietary armor solutions as industries seek higher performance and reliability. CPS is well-positioned to offer solutions to current and new customers as these demands grow.

Market Demand: Despite the completion of a significant armor order for the U.S. Navy in Q2 2024, demand in other product lines has significantly increased, supported by additional funding from the federal government’s Small Business Innovative Research (SBIR) program.

Operational Challenges: The company faces challenges related to inflation and tariffs, which have impacted costs. CPS has been able to pass on most cost increases to customers, but there is uncertainty about the ability to continue doing so in the future.

Supply Chain and Inflation: While CPS does not rely on raw materials from conflict regions like Ukraine, Russia, Israel, or Gaza, there could be indirect impacts on supply chain and inflationary issues. The company has seen some impact from tariffs and inflation on its cost structure.

Competitive Landscape: The strength of the US dollar versus the Japanese yen can impact CPS's pricing competitiveness against its major competitor in Japan, affecting the company's ability to raise prices to offset increasing costs.

SEC Filing: CPS TECHNOLOGIES CORP/DE/ [ CPSH ] - 10-Q - Aug. 01, 2025

data.title: CORNING INC /NY SEC 10-Q Report
data.description:
Corning Inc., a leading innovator in materials science, has released its Form 10-Q report for the second quarter of 2025. The report highlights significant financial growth and operational achievements, driven by strong demand in key segments such as Optical Communications and Specialty Materials. The company has also outlined its strategic initiatives aimed at sustaining profitability and growth in the coming quarters.

Financial Highlights

Net Sales: $3,862 million, an increase of 19% compared to the same period in 2024, primarily driven by an increase in sales for optical communication products.

Gross Margin: $1,392 million, a 47% increase compared to the same period in 2024, with the gross margin percentage improving to 36% from 29% due to actions taken by management to improve profitability.

Income Before Income Taxes: $584 million, a significant increase from $172 million in the same period in 2024, driven by increases in gross margin and gains on translated earnings contracts.

Net Income Attributable to Corning Incorporated: $469 million, reflecting a substantial increase from $104 million in the same period in 2024, with an effective tax rate of 14.4%.

Core Net Income: $523 million, a 29% increase compared to the same period in 2024, primarily due to higher segment net income in Optical Communications.

Core Earnings Per Share: $0.60, a 28% increase compared to $0.47 in the same period in 2024, primarily as a result of the changes in core net income.

Business Highlights

Revenue Segments - Optical Communications: The Optical Communications segment experienced significant growth, with net sales increasing by $453 million for the three months ended June 30, 2025, driven by strong demand for Generative AI products and datacenter interconnect products.

Revenue Segments - Display: The Display segment saw a decrease in net sales by $116 million for the three months ended June 30, 2025, primarily due to reduced panel maker utilization, although pricing actions in the second half of 2024 helped offset some of the impact.

Revenue Segments - Specialty Materials: Specialty Materials segment net sales increased by $44 million for the three months ended June 30, 2025, attributed to strong demand for premium glass for mobile devices.

Revenue Segments - Automotive: The Automotive segment experienced a decline in net sales by $19 million for the three months ended June 30, 2025, due to continued softness in light-duty and heavy-duty markets, particularly in Europe and North America.

Revenue Segments - Life Sciences: Life Sciences segment net sales remained consistent with the previous year, showing stability in this area.

Geographical Performance: The company faced challenges in the European and North American automotive markets, impacting the Automotive segment's performance.

Future Outlook: Corning expects core net sales of approximately $4.2 billion for the third quarter of 2025, indicating continued growth expectations.

Operational Strategy: Management has taken actions to improve profitability, including raising prices, restoring productivity levels, and normalizing inventory levels, which contributed to an increase in gross margin.

Forward-Looking Statements: The company anticipates continued growth in its Enterprise and Carrier businesses within the Optical Communications segment, driven by demand for advanced technology products.

SEC Filing: CORNING INC /NY [ GLW ] - 10-Q - Aug. 01, 2025

THE NORTH AMERICAN INCOME TRUST PLC (the 'Company')

Total Voting Rights

In conformity with Disclosure Guidance and Transparency Rule 5.6.1 we would like to notify the market of the following:

As at 31 July 2025, The North American Income Trust plc's capital consisted of 126,923,569 ordinary shares of 5p, of which 7,868,681 ordinary shares were held in Treasury. Shares held in Treasury do not possess voting rights. On a poll, members have one vote for every share held. Therefore, the total number of voting rights in The North American Income Trust plc is 119,054,888.

The above figure 119,054,888 may be used by shareholders as the denominator for the calculations by which they will determine if they are required to notify their interest in, or a change to their interest in, the voting rights of The North American Income Trust plc under the FCA's Disclosure Guidance and Transparency Rules.

data.title: Cocoa falls as investors hold out for US tariff relief
data.description:
Cocoa futures on ICE fell on Friday as investors held out hope the Trump administration would exempt some commodities from the slew of tariffs it has imposed on dozens of trading partners.

Nearly the entire U.S. landmass is unsuitable for growing tropical commodities like coffee and cocoa.

COCOA

* London cocoa futures were down 1.5% at 5,435 pounds a metric ton at 1429 GMT, having closed up 3.5% on Thursday.

* Malaysia said on Friday the U.S., which has slapped a 19% tariff on the country, is open to not imposing tariffs on cocoa, rubber and palm oil, though a deal is still being finalised.

* The U.S. Commerce Secretary had said on Tuesday commodities not grown in the U.S. could be exempt from tariffs if deals with producing countries are reached.

* Commerzbank noted, however, that in the absence of trade deal talks, tariffs of 15% will apply to Ivory Coast and Ghana, which produce about 60% of the world's chocolate-making beans.

* Elsewhere, dealers said sub-optimal weather and better than expected results from chocolate major Hershey are limiting losses in cocoa.

* New York futures fell 1.4% at $7,633 a ton, having closed up 3.3% on Thursday.

COFFEE

* Arabica coffee futures were little changed at $2.9605 per lb, having closed up 0.8% on Thursday.

* The U.S. on Wednesday slapped a 50% tariff on top coffee grower Brazil, effective August 6, though Brazilian exporters and U.S. sector lobbyists are continuing to push for exemptions.

* Coffee prices are expected to rise short-term if the 50% tariff goes ahead, with a scramble for stocks and a major upheaval in global trade flows likely as supplies are redirected.

* The U.S. imports about a third of its coffee from Brazil, but tariffs on the country are seen as political rather than economic in nature, meaning they are more likely to stick.

* Robusta coffee rose 0.8% to $3,358 a metric ton, having closed down 0.4% on Thursday.

* Indonesia's Sumatra robusta coffee bean exports in June reached 53,870.2 tons, up 390% year-on-year, data showed.

* Indonesia is the world's second-largest robusta exporter.

SUGAR

* Raw sugar fell 0.7% to 16.23 cents per lb.

* Sugar production in Brazil's key center-south region rose 15.07% in the first half of July when compared to the year-earlier period, industry group UNICA said.

* While white sugar lost 0.4% to $465.90 a ton.

data.title: US ISM MANUFACTURING REPORT: LAYOFFS WERE PRIMARY MEASURE IN JULY, AN INDICATION THAT STAFF SHRINKING CONTINUES TO BE URGENT
Stocks Are Sure to Weather Trump's Tariff Blitz. The Economy May Not. — Barrons.com
data.description:
By Martin Baccardax

Stocks are moving lower in early Friday trading, setting up a weak start to what is historically one of the toughest months of the year for global markets, following the latest slate of tariffs unveiled by President Donald Trump.

The levies, which will take effect on Aug. 7, will effectively raise the effective tariff rate to around 17%, according to Capital Economics. That is a huge increase from the 3% level that was in place last year and the highest overall burden since the early 1930s.

Stocks are likely to weather the latest tariff blitz, however, as the president has carved out framework deals with major trading partners in Europe, the United Kingdom, and Japan. Administration officials have signaled they will probably extend talks with China that could eventually result in a lower overall rate on goods from the world's second-biggest economy.

Tariffs, however, are ultimately paid by U.S. importers and passed on to U.S. consumers in the form of higher prices. And that could make the economy, which is now growing at half the pace it was last year, more vulnerable to a tariff shock than the stock market.

The S&P 500 is sitting on solid gains for the year. It has risen more than 27% from its early April lows in a searing rally powered in part by outsize gains for mega-cap tech stocks. Second-quarter earnings, despite the tariff uncertainty, have been solid.

S&P 500 companies are on pace to see their collective profits for the six months through June rise nearly 10% from last year to just over $540 billion. Similar gains are expected for the back half of the year.

Apple, Amazon.com, Microsoft, Alphabet, and Meta Platforms all posted better-than-expected earnings for the June quarter. Every one of them reiterated plans for enormous capital spending, or increased those numbers, as they seek to gain from the rollout of artificial technologies.

That bodes well for Nvidia, which closes out the summer reporting season later this month.

"Nvidia sits at the center of the tech investment universe," said Charu Chanana, chief investment strategist at Saxo Bank. "With nearly every major platform raising AI-related capex, expectations for Nvidia's second-quarter earnings in late August are extremely high."

The larger risk from the president's latest salvo is how it will affect the broader economy. Growth over the first half of the year is already markedly lower than it was over the same period in 2024. Data on spending, investment, and the labor market are trending in the wrong direction. Inflation is ticking higher, as well.

Details from the president's executive order, meanwhile, indicate that countries can avoid the new, higher tariff rates if goods are placed in transit by Aug. 7 and arrive in the U.S. by Oct. 5.

"That could trigger a brief wave of tariff front-running and should limit the speed of the pass-through to U.S. consumer prices," said Stephen Brown, deputy chief North America economist at Capital Economics.

But that will also weigh heavily on third-quarter gross domestic product. Any surge in imports will lower the overall reading in much the same way as they did over the first three months of the year, when businesses rushed to bring in goods ahead of the president's April 2 tariff announcement. Imports are subtracted from total economic output in calculating GDP.

James Knightley, chief international economist for the U.S. at ING, notes that both consumer spending and business momentum slowed over the second quarter. He says the "consumer story is critical to the outlook, given it accounts for around 70% of economic activity as measured by GDP."

He cited "anxiety over the price impact of tariffs on household spending power, worries about the outlook for jobs and uncertainty caused by big swings in asset prices and household wealth" as factors contributing to the slowdown. All of those are likely to extend into the final months of the year, lead by weakness in the job market.

The Bureau of Labor Statistics said only 73,000 new jobs were created last month, the smallest tally since October. Revisions to the data for May and June lopped another 258,000 jobs from the official payroll tally.

Challenger Gray & Christmas's benchmark report of corporate layoffs, published Thursday, showed job cuts rose by 29% last month to just over 62,000. It said that brought the year-to-date tally to just over 806,000, the highest since 2020.

St. Louis Fed data also suggests that 113,000 manufacturing jobs have been lost over the past year, while the Institute for Supply Management's July benchmark reading of activity in the sector contracted at the fastest pace in 9 months.

Forcing already cautious consumers to deal with higher prices as the job market weakens is a dangerous economic mix. The prospect of the damage it could cause is likely to shift focus firmly back toward potential rare cuts by the Federal Reserve, which has thus far resisted unprecedented pressures to reduce borrowing costs.

Bets that rates will be cut accelerated in early Friday trading following the July payroll report and the publication of arguments for easing from Fed governors Michelle Bowman and Christopher Waller.

The CME FedWatch Tool now pegs the odds of a September cut at 80%.

"Now that the bulk of earnings season is behind us, the market's next focal point is the August Jackson Hole speech, where [Federal Reserve Chairman] Powell will have the option to prepare the markets for a fall rate cut if he chooses," said Glen Smith, chief investment officer at GDS Wealth Management.

Write to Martin Baccardax at martin.baccardax@barrons.com

This content was created by Barron's, which is operated by Dow Jones & Co. Barron's is published independently from Dow Jones Newswires and The Wall Street Journal.

data.title: Wall Street's Fear Gauge Shoots Higher, Marking End to Tranquil Streak — WSJ
data.description:
By Roshan Fernandez

Wall Street's "fear gauge" climbed Friday as the market jolted awake after a tranquil period recently.

The Cboe Volatility index, which uses options to measure short-term expectations for how volatile the S&P 500 will be, jumped 19% to trade at nearly 20. That is still a relatively modest level compared to periods of acute market stress.

The Nasdaq Composite is set to snap a streak of 19 trading days without a 1% move in either direction, the longest such streak since July 2021, according to Dow Jones Market Data.

The S&P 500 is on pace to end a 26-day streak of sub-1% moves, the longest since October. Both indexes sank Friday after a weak July jobs report and President Trump's revamped tariff plan.

This item is part of a Wall Street Journal live coverage event. The full stream can be found by searching P/WSJL (WSJ Live Coverage).

data.title: Utah Housing to Sell $65.7M of Multifamily Residential Development Bonds
data.description:
By Chris Wack

The Utah Housing Corporation will sell $65.7 million of bonds to help finance a new multifamily residential development in Salt Lake City.

The Series 2025 Multifamily Housing Revenue Bond offering are being issued to finance a loan to Liberty Corner Associates, a Utah limited liability company, to enable the borrower to pay for the cost of buying, constructing and equipping a multifamily residential development, according to a document posted on MuniOS.

The development will contain 200 rental housing units, and may include ancillary uses such as parking, community space and other functionally related uses. Construction of the property is expected to begin upon issuance of the bonds, and will be completed in about 26 months.

Interest on the bonds is payable starting on March 1 and again on Sept 1. Interest earned is excluded from gross income for federal income tax purposes, and isn't a specific preference item for purposes of the federal alternative minimum tax. Interest is also exempt from Utah individual taxes.

The securities are limited obligations of the Utah Housing Corporation. The bonds will mature on Sept. 1, 2045.

The Utah Housing Corporation was created as an independent body politic and corporate, to assure an adequate source of capital for housing low- and moderate-income persons living in Utah.

Moody's has issued a Aa1 ratings on the bonds.

Wells Fargo Securities is the underwriter.

Write to Chris Wack at chris.wack@wsj.com

data.title: THERMO FISHER SCIENTIFIC INC. SEC 10-Q Report
data.description:
Thermo Fisher Scientific Inc., a global leader in serving science, has released its Form 10-Q report for the second quarter of 2025. The report highlights the company's robust financial performance and strategic business initiatives, underscoring its commitment to innovation and operational excellence in the life sciences industry.

Financial Highlights

Total Revenues: $10,855 million, reflecting a 3% increase compared to the same period in 2024, driven by growth in the pharma and biotech market.

Operating Income: $1,834 million, with an operating income margin of 16.9%, slightly down from 17.3% in the previous year due to unfavorable business mix and strategic investments.

Net Income: $1,618 million, showing an increase from $1,553 million in the previous year, supported by strong productivity improvements.

Net Income Attributable to Thermo Fisher Scientific Inc.: $1,617 million, up from $1,548 million in the previous year, indicating a 6% growth.

Diluted EPS: $4.28, compared to $4.04 in the previous year, reflecting a 6% increase, driven by improved operational efficiencies and strategic investments.

Business Highlights

Revenue Segments: The Life Sciences Solutions segment saw a 6% increase in revenue for the second quarter of 2025, driven by strong performance in the bioproduction business. The Analytical Instruments segment experienced a 3% decline in revenue, primarily due to decreased demand for equipment and instrumentation. Specialty Diagnostics reported a 2% increase in revenue, with growth in the transplant diagnostics business. The Laboratory Products and Biopharma Services segment achieved a 4% revenue increase, supported by growth in the pharma services business and the research and safety market channel.

Geographical Performance: During the second quarter of 2025, sales grew in North America and Europe, while sales declined in the Asia-Pacific region, including China. The company noted increased demand from pharma and biotech customers in North America and Europe, but faced challenges in China due to market headwinds.

Operational Efficiency: The company continues to focus on productivity improvements through its Practical Process Improvement (PPI) business system, which includes cost efficiencies, global sourcing initiatives, and restructuring actions such as headcount reductions and facility consolidations.

Future Outlook: The company expects to continue executing its growth strategy, which includes high-impact innovation, strengthening customer partnerships, and leveraging its commercial engine. The company anticipates further strategic investments to enhance commercial capabilities and customer experience.

Segment Income Margins: The Life Sciences Solutions segment maintained a stable income margin of 36.8% in the second quarter of 2025, while the Analytical Instruments segment saw a decrease in income margin to 18.8% due to tariffs and strategic investments. Specialty Diagnostics improved its income margin to 27.0%, driven by productivity gains.

Strategic Investments: The company is making targeted investments to enhance commercial capabilities, including expanding geographic sales reach, e-commerce platforms, and marketing initiatives. These investments are aimed at improving customer experience and operational infrastructure.

Productivity Improvements: The company achieved strong productivity improvements, which helped offset some of the unfavorable business mix and strategic investment impacts. These improvements are part of the company's ongoing efforts to enhance operational efficiency and cost management.

SEC Filing: THERMO FISHER SCIENTIFIC INC. [ TMO ] - 10-Q - Aug. 01, 2025

data.title: DOMINION ENERGY, INC SEC 10-Q Report
data.description:
Dominion Energy, Inc., a leading provider of regulated electric and gas utility services, has released its Form 10-Q report for the second quarter of 2025. The report highlights significant growth in both financial and operational metrics, underscoring the company's robust performance and strategic focus on expanding its renewable energy portfolio.

Financial Highlights

Dominion Energy, Inc. reported impressive financial results for the three months ended June 30, 2025, showcasing substantial growth across key performance indicators:

  • Operating Revenue: $3,810 million, up from $3,486 million in the same period in 2024, indicating a strong increase in sales.
  • Income from Operations: $1,096 million, compared to $805 million in the same period in 2024, reflecting improved operational efficiency.
  • Net Income Including Noncontrolling Interests: $814 million, a significant rise from $563 million in the same period in 2024, demonstrating enhanced profitability.
  • Net Income Attributable to Dominion Energy: $760 million, up from $563 million in the same period in 2024, highlighting strong financial performance.
  • EPS - Basic: $0.88, compared to $0.64 in the same period in 2024, reflecting higher earnings per share.
  • EPS - Diluted: $0.88, compared to $0.64 in the same period in 2024, indicating consistent growth in diluted earnings per share.

Business Highlights

Dominion Energy's operational performance for the second quarter of 2025 was marked by several key achievements and strategic initiatives:

  • Revenue Segments: The company's operating revenue is primarily derived from regulated electric sales, which include residential, commercial, industrial, and government sectors. Nonregulated electric sales and regulated gas sales also contribute to the revenue stream.
  • Geographical Performance: Dominion Energy serves primarily electric utility customers in Virginia, North Carolina, and South Carolina. The company is also a significant producer of carbon-free electricity in New England.
  • Sales Units: The company reported significant volumes in natural gas and electricity derivatives, with natural gas fixed price contracts totaling 35 bcf for current and 17 bcf for noncurrent, and electricity fixed price contracts totaling 14 million MWh for current and 32 million MWh for noncurrent.
  • Future Outlook: Dominion Energy is focused on expanding its operations in regulated offshore wind and solar power, aiming to maintain its position as a leading developer and operator in these sectors. The company is also adapting to new tax credits for nuclear and clean fuel production, which are expected to impact future financial performance.

SEC Filing: DOMINION ENERGY, INC [ D ] - 10-Q - Aug. 01, 2025

data.title: Iterum Therapeutics PLC expected to post a loss of 11 cents a share - Earnings Preview
data.description:
• Iterum Therapeutics PLC is expected to show no change in quarterly revenue when it reports results on August 5 for the period ending June 30 2025

  • ​LSEG's mean analyst estimate for Iterum Therapeutics PLC is for a loss of 11 cents per share.

  • The current average analyst rating on the shares is "buy" and the breakdown of recommendations is 2 "strong buy" or "buy," no "hold" and no "sell" or "strong sell."

  • The mean earnings estimate of analysts had risen by about 3.3% in the last three months. ​

  • Wall Street's median 12-month price target for Iterum Therapeutics PLC is $7.00, about 89.6% above its last closing price of $0.73
